title: Gleam stdlib I/O and URI wrappers + sample workflow e2e unlock
purpose: Complete the gleam_stdlib wrapper coverage with I/O, utility, and URI functions. Un-ignore the sample_workflow_e2e test to prove end-to-end Gleam workflow execution through beamr.
task: Implement 13 remaining gleam_stdlib and uri_string BIFs, then un-ignore and verify the sample workflow e2e test.
boundaries: SHALL NOT implement full RFC 3986 URI parsing — stub uri_string:parse/1 with approximate splitting
SHALL route print/println through the IoSink trait from B-039
SHALL mark uri_string stubs as approximations

requirements.title: gleam_stdlib I/O and utility wrappers (7 functions)
requirements.spec: Register under gleam_stdlib: is_null/1, list/5, map_get/2, print/1, print_error/1, println/1, println_error/1.

requirements.title: URI wrappers + uri_string stubs (6 functions)
requirements.spec: Register gleam_stdlib: parse_query/1, percent_decode/1, percent_encode/1, uri_parse/1. Register uri_string: parse/1, dissect_query/1. URI stubs are approximate.
acceptance: uri_string:parse(<<"https://example.com/path?q=1">>) returns map with scheme, host, path, query

requirements.title: sample_workflow e2e test — un-ignore and verify
requirements.spec: Remove #[ignore] from sample_workflow_run_completes_end_to_end. Verify the test passes with all stdlib BIFs implemented.
acceptance: Test loads all .beam files from test-workflows/sample/
All modules load with zero or warn-only unresolved imports
Return value is a well-formed tuple
